projects.name: Hierarch
projects.description: React task management app with drag-and-drop, focus timer, and today view
projects.notes: Mobile responsiveness is solid now. The unified drawer system works on mobile as a full-screen slide-up with iOS-style back/close navigation, projects open in drawers, and task push works within project context. Drag-to-reorder is gated behind a toggle on mobile so scrolling works cleanly. Recent Progress feed shows the latest 20 items regardless of age. The onboarding, project details, and new task dialogs all render as mobile-friendly drawers. Next: GDPR cleanup (account deletion, data export, retention policies).

projects.name: Kinlo
projects.description: iOS health app for desk workers. Tracks workouts, water intake, and movement breaks tied to work hours. Streak-based habit reinforcement. Social gamification via friend leaderboard and challenges (Phase 2). Phase 1 MVP complete.
projects.notes: Major visual redesign complete. The app now matches a dark-mode-first design system with true black backgrounds, lime green accent, 3-ring progress circle, Quick Actions cards with + buttons, and a leaderboard preview on the home screen. XP scoring, individual goal celebrations, streak tracking, and notification triggers are all wired up. A full gamification playbook (GAMIFICATION.md) outlines the retention strategy. Next: implement streak calendar, level titles, variable XP, and the Day 2/3 notification sequence.

projects.name: NourishAI
projects.notes: NourishAI Expo prototype is complete and running in the iOS simulator with auth, onboarding, AI meal generation, water tracking, and product scanner. EAS Build kept failing due to Reanimated/Worklets version conflicts. Decision made to pivot to native SwiftUI using the existing Kinlo Xcode project instead of fighting Expo build issues. The Kinlo project already has auth, onboarding, Supabase, and MVVM architecture. Next session: adapt Kinlo's SwiftUI codebase to include NourishAI's 6 lifestyle pillars (Eat, Move, Hydrate, Rest, Scan, Rhythm) with AI meal plans, macro tracking, food logging (barcode + photo + manual), and EU-banned product scanner.

projects.name: RockPass
projects.notes: Full Phase 1 scaffold is complete and running in Xcode on iPhone 16 Pro simulator. Built 5 screens (Home, Gyms, My Pass, Gear, Account) in Expo Router with a custom elevated center tab bar, Google Maps integration with a live API key restricted to com.rockpass.app, and a 909-line Figma plugin ready to generate all screens in the RockPass Figma file. Next: run the Figma plugin, wire up real gym data via Google Places API, and build the auth layer.

projects.name: hierarch-fos
projects.notes: Hierarch FOS marketing site is live at tryhierarch.com. All login/signup links now point to hierarchical.app. Feature sections simplified from 10 total cards to 7, integrations updated to reflect Linear + Figma (live) and Jira (coming soon), testimonials cleaned up. Next: connect tryhierarch.com domain properly, add real pricing tiers, build out remaining placeholder pages.

projects.name: knitlab
projects.notes: KnitLab MVP is built with a 23-pattern stitch library (7 categories), SVG chart renderer, written instruction generator, manual stitch counter with keyboard shortcuts and localStorage persistence, image-based stitch counter with draggable grid overlay, and gauge calculator. The app builds clean on Next.js 16 + TypeScript + Tailwind. Next: deploy to Vercel, add more patterns, and consider PDF export for printed instructions.

projects.name: mission-control
projects.description: Claude Code command center — live dashboard tracking projects, skills, MCPs, and session history
projects.notes: Mission Control now has a Launch button on every project card that opens the project in Cursor with full context. The API route reads the project's mission-log.md and .mc.json, writes combined context into .cursor/rules/mission-context.mdc, then opens Cursor. Next: scaffold mission-log.md templates across projects and test the end-to-end launch flow.

projects.name: modular-email-builder
projects.notes: All 70 variations from the email anatomy doc are implemented across 11 section types. The UI now uses claymorphic floating panels: a single left panel that switches between a Layouts browser and a per-block Settings view with variation picker, element visibility toggles, and content fields. Drop indicators use manual bounding-rect hit testing and zero-layout-shift rendering. Next: rich text editing, image URL support, and Constant Contact export.

projects.name: portfolio
projects.notes: Portfolio is live with password gate removed, tile metrics stripped from all 6 case studies in favor of plain text topMetrics, sidebar back button removed, sidebar-to-content gap widened, prompt-to-email has a new hero background image with color overlay, case study dark theme text brightened, and glass cards dim slightly on hover. Dead code remains: /gate page, verify-code API, MetricCard component, BackButton component.

projects.name: prouxkit
projects.notes: All 9 tools built and styled with light mode Coolors-inspired design. Custom wrench logo with favicon. Deployed to Vercel with prouxkit.com domain configured.

projects.name: radar
projects.notes: Radar is fully migrated from Supabase to Neon Postgres via the Vercel integration, freeing up a Supabase project slot. All 10 API routes rewritten to use @neondatabase/serverless with raw SQL, 436 companies and 7 active job listings migrated, and the app is deployed and scanning on Neon. Next: delete the old Supabase project and optionally set up a custom domain.

projects.name: showcase
projects.notes: Showcase rebuilt as a VS Code-style code editor portfolio for the arrivia Sr. Product Designer interview. 15 project files across 4 folders with tab management, lightbox, and stats. Images added for mWeb Gamification, Mobile Email Editor, BrandKit, Prompt-to-Email, Contacts Upload, Spicy Resume, StudioFrame, and Hierarch. Live at showcase.aaroncougle.com.

projects.name: spicy-resume
projects.notes: Redesigned with modern neo-brutalist/flat blend UI. Tabbed sidebar (Resumes + Work History), inline block editing via sidebar panel, per-card export/delete menus, tailor-to-job popover. Supabase document storage is live with RLS, auto-migration from localStorage on first login. PDF export downloads directly. Next: re-enable auth gates, Stripe payments, work history Supabase sync.

projects.name: studioframe
projects.notes: StudioFrame is now a web app at app.studiofra.me with the marketing site at studiofra.me. Converted from Electron to browser-based with a Supabase auth shim, browser-native recording via getDisplayMedia, and graceful degradation for desktop-only features. Fixed a persistent iframe gap bug caused by 100vh shifting in browsers (switched to 100dvh). Background tab renamed to Pattern with rotate control hidden for images. Next: payment/subscription gating and Apple OAuth.

projects.name: website-analyzer
projects.description: Next.js tool for analyzing and auditing websites
